summary: FQ-13P2 turns the reserved UI hook ids from the FQ-13P0 audit into authored deliberate placeholders and consumes the first live surface. scripts/gen_ui_placeholders.py generates fifteen recognizable 32x32 RGBA glyphs into art/generated/ui/ (framed slots normal/selected/invalid, health/attunement orb rings + fill mask, dock backplate, six nav buttons backpack/helmet/hall/skill-star/scroll/gear, and valid/invalid drag cursors) with one shared palette + 1px border language, nearest-friendly, deterministic and idempotent; the PNGs are committed. hud.gd frames the hotbar slots with slot_inventory/slot_inventory_selected via _make_slot_style -> StyleBoxTexture, falling back to the original StyleBoxFlat when the image is absent (missing art is never an error), and widens the slot StyleBox var types. asset_audit.py becomes UI-aware: UI_CONSUMED (slot_inventory*) report LIVE, the rest are PLACEHOLDER_AUTHORED (reserved for the HUD redesign, not orphans), and the reserved-hook report splits authored vs still-missing; the audit is 0 findings, 0 data bugs with all 15 UI at 32x32. Three fq13p2_* smoke checks prove the placeholders load through the ui convention, the live slot consumes the StyleBoxTexture frame, and a missing ui id yields null plus the flat fallback. Suite 288 -> 291. The orb/nav-button/dock/cursor placeholders are authored + reserved for the future HUD redesign (the current HUD uses bars, not orbs, and has no nav dock).
scope: FQ-13P2 per the FQ-13P arc - deliberate generated UI placeholder art for the fifteen reserved hook ids, the first live consumption (hotbar slot frames), and UI-aware auditing. No HUD redesign, no gameplay change.
next_action: FQ-13P3: player full-body cosmetic pool (design locked). Then FQ-13P4: opening variant-vs-animation distinction + block/item variation follow-through.
